heading: Tax discrimination against motor carrier transportation property
label: 14502 Tax discrimination against motor carrier transportation property
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by .section 11503a of this titlePub. L. 104–88, § 102(a)
Effective DateSection effective , except as otherwise provided in , see , set out as a note under .Jan. 1, 1996Pub. L. 104–88section 2 of Pub. L. 104–88section 1301 of this title
amendments: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by .section 11503a of this titlePub. L. 104–88, § 102(a)

children.heading: .—Assessment
children.label: 1 .—Assessment
children.text: The term “assessment” means valuation for a property tax levied by a taxing district.

children.heading: .—Assessment jurisdiction
children.label: 2 .—Assessment jurisdiction
children.text: The term “assessment jurisdiction” means a geographical area in a State used in determining the assessed value of property for ad valorem taxation.

children.heading: .—Motor carrier transportation property
children.label: 3 .—Motor carrier transportation property
children.text: The term “motor carrier transportation property” means property, as defined by the Secretary, owned or used by a motor carrier providing transportation in interstate commerce whether or not such transportation is subject to jurisdiction under subchapter I of chapter 135.

children.heading: .—Commercial and industrial property
children.label: 4 .—Commercial and industrial property
children.text: The term “commercial and industrial property” means property, other than transportation property and land used primarily for agricultural purposes or timber growing, devoted to a commercial or industrial use, and subject to a property tax levy.

children.heading: .—Excessive valuation of property
children.label: 1 .—Excessive valuation of property
children.text: Assess motor carrier transportation property at a value that has a higher ratio to the true market value of the motor carrier transportation property than the ratio that the assessed value of other commercial and industrial property in the same assessment jurisdiction has to the true market value of the other commercial and industrial property.

children.heading: .—Tax on assessment
children.label: 2 .—Tax on assessment
children.text: Levy or collect a tax on an assessment that may not be made under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Ad valorem tax
children.label: 3 .—Ad valorem tax
children.text: Levy or collect an ad valorem property tax on motor carrier transportation property at a tax rate that exceeds the tax rate applicable to commercial and industrial property in the same assessment jurisdiction.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Notwithstanding and without regard to the amount in controversy or citizenship of the parties, a district court of the United States has jurisdiction, concurrent with other jurisdiction of courts of the United States and the States, to prevent a violation of subsection (b) of this section.section 1341 of title 28

children.heading: .—Limitation in relief
children.label: 2 .—Limitation in relief
children.text: Relief may be granted under this subsection only if the ratio of assessed value to true market value of motor carrier transportation property exceeds, by at least 5 percent, the ratio of assessed value to true market value of other commercial and industrial property in the same assessment jurisdiction.

children.heading: .—Burden of proof
children.label: 3 .—Burden of proof
children.text: The burden of proof in determining assessed value and true market value is governed by State law.

children.label: A
children.text: an assessment of the motor carrier transportation property at a value that has a higher ratio to the true market value of the motor carrier transportation property than the assessment value of all other property subject to a property tax levy in the assessment jurisdiction has to the true market value of all such other property; and

children.label: B
children.text: the collection of ad valorem property tax on the motor carrier transportation property at a tax rate that exceeds the tax ratio rate applicable to taxable property in the taxing district.
